name: Chicken Potpies
recipeIngredient: 1/2 teaspoon dried sage, marjoram or thyme, crushed
3 cups cooked chicken or turkey, cubed
recipeInstructions:
Prepare the pastry and set aside. Cook the peas and carrots according to package directions; drain. In a saucepan, cook onion and mushrooms in butter or margarine until tender. Stir in flour, salt, sage, marjoram or thyme, and pepper. Add chicken broth and milk all at once. Cook and stir until thickened and bubbly. Stir in drained peas and carrots, chicken or turkey, parsley, and pimiento; heat until bubbly. Pour the chicken mixture into six 10-ounce round casseroles (or use a 12 X 7 1/2 X 2-inch baking dish). Roll the pastry into a 15 X 10-inch rectangle. Cut into six 5-inch circles and place atop the 10-ounce casseroles. Alternatively, roll the pastry into a 13 X 9-inch rectangle and place over the 12 X 7 1/2 X 2-inch baking dish. Flute the edges of the pastry and cut slits in the top for steam to escape. Bake in a 450°F oven for 12-15 minutes or until the pastry is golden brown.
Biscuit-Topped Chicken Potpies: Prepare as above, except omit the pastry. Cut 1 package (6) refrigerated biscuits into quarters and arrange atop the bubbly chicken mixture in casseroles or baking dish. Bake in a 400°F oven for about 15 minutes or until the biscuits are golden.
